subject: Astronomy & Space
title: The Gaia Mission: Mapping a Billion Stars
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Gaia Mission: Mapping a Billion Stars.

hook.text: ESA's Gaia spacecraft is mapping the positions and motions of nearly 2 billion stars in our galaxy.

buildup.text: Launched in 2013, it measures each star's position, distance, speed, and brightness with unprecedented accuracy.

discovery.text: Gaia revealed streams of stars from ancient dwarf galaxies that the Milky Way consumed billions of years ago.

twist.text: It also found that the Milky Way's disk is warped and twisted—not flat as previously assumed.

climax.text: Gaia's data releases have transformed galactic astronomy. Thousands of papers use its measurements each year.

punchline.text: Two billion stars mapped. Our galaxy's full biography is being written.

quiz.question: What surprising feature of the Milky Way did Gaia reveal?
options: The disk is warped and twisted, not perfectly flat
The galaxy has no central black hole
It is actually an elliptical galaxy, not a spiral
